changes.value:
## What

When a task moves between columns (via drag, API, or any other process), the board's computed `percent_complete` field does not emit an `entity-field-changed` event. The UI never learns the board changed.

**Backend fix** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/mv.rs`): After `MoveTask` persists the position change, re-read the board entity (which triggers the `board-percent-complete` aggregate compute) and emit `entity-field-changed` for the board entity with the updated `percent_complete` value. This ensures any process that moves a task â€” UI drag, MCP agent, CLI â€” triggers a board-level update.

**Frontend fix** (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-progress.tsx` + `nav-bar.tsx`): Replace the custom `BoardProgress` radial chart that reads from `board.summary` with a `<Field>` rendering the board's `percent_complete` field from the entity store. The existing `entity-field-changed` handler in `App.tsx` already updates entities in the store, so the Field will re-render automatically.

### Files to modify
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/mv.rs` â€” emit board `entity-field-changed` after move
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.tsx` â€” use `<Field>` for percent_complete instead of custom `BoardProgress`
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-progress.tsx` â€” may be deleted or simplified to a display component

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Moving a task to done emits `entity-field-changed` for the board entity
- [ ] Board progress updates without UI calling refresh
- [ ] Progress works when tasks are moved by any process, not just UI drag
- [ ] Progress is rendered as a Field, not a bespoke component

## Tests
- [ ] Rust integration test: move task to done column â†’ read board â†’ `percent_complete.percent` increases
- [ ] Rust integration test: move task out of done â†’ `percent_complete.percent` decreases
- [ ] Run: `cd swissarmyhammer-kanban && cargo nextest run move_task_updates_board_progress`
- [ ] Run: `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` â€” no UI regressions"
Board percent_complete recomputes and emits on task move
